explanation: Honey extract is derived from raw honey and provides flavonoids, phenolic acids, enzymes, and amino acids with antioxidant, humectant, and mild antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated by most skin types and is used in hydrating and soothing formulas for its moisture-binding and protective benefits.

Honey extract — the asterisk indicates organic certification — provides flavonoids, phenolic antioxidants, and humectant sugars from raw honey with moisture-binding and mild antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated and used in hydrating and soothing formulas.

Honey extract — the asterisk indicates organic certification — provides flavonoids, phenolic antioxidants, and humectant sugars from raw honey with moisture-binding and mild antimicrobial properties. It is well-tolerated and used in hydrating and soothing formulas.

Hordeum distichon (two-row barley) extract is a grain-derived botanical providing beta-glucan, phenolic antioxidants, and amino acids with hydrating, soothing, and free-radical-scavenging properties. It is well-tolerated and used in protective and calming formulas for its antioxidant and moisture-supporting content.

Houttuynia cordata (dokudami/fish mint) extract is a traditional East Asian medicinal botanical rich in quercetin, quercitrin, and polyphenols with documented antibacterial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties. It is well-tolerated and widely used in K-beauty formulas for blemish-prone and reactive skin for its ability to calm inflammation and inhibit acne-causing bacteria.

Hyacinthus orientalis (hyacinth) extract provides flavonoid antioxidants with free-radical-scavenging and skin-conditioning properties. It is generally well-tolerated and used in protective formulas for its antioxidant content, though individuals with known floral allergies should patch-test.

Hyaluronic acid is a naturally occurring glycosaminoglycan that can hold up to 1,000 times its weight in water, making it the most powerful humectant in skincare for attracting and retaining moisture in the stratum corneum. It is exceptionally well-tolerated by all skin types including sensitive, reactive, and acne-prone — the gold-standard hydrating ingredient across all modern skincare formulas.

Hyaluronic acid crosspolymer-2-sodium is a crosslinked, network-forming hyaluronic acid derivative that forms a more substantive hydrating film on the skin surface than standard HA, providing longer-lasting moisture retention and a plumping effect. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, used in high-performance hydrating serums and moisturizers.

Hyaluronic acid/polyglutamic acid crosspolymer is a hybrid network of two powerful humectants — hyaluronic acid and polyglutamic acid — that together provide enhanced moisture attraction and retention compared to either ingredient alone, with polyglutamic acid forming a surface film that slows HA evaporation. It is well-tolerated and used in advanced hydrating formulas for its complementary dual-humectant action.

Hydrocolloid is a class of water-absorbing hydrophilic polymers (typically a blend of gelatin, pectin, and carboxymethylcellulose) best known for their use in acne patch technology, where they absorb fluid from active blemishes to accelerate healing and prevent picking. In patches, they create a moist wound-healing environment that is well-tolerated and reduces post-inflammatory hyperpigmentation.

Hydrogen dimethicone is a modified silicone containing Si-H groups that allow it to function as a reactive crosslinker and silicone emulsifier in addition to providing the smoothing properties of standard dimethicone. It is well-tolerated and non-sensitizing, used in silicone elastomer and emulsifier systems in lightweight moisturizers and primers.

Hydrogenated castor oil (castor wax) is castor oil that has been fully hydrogenated to produce a hard, waxy solid used as a thickener and structuring agent in balms, lipsticks, and anhydrous formulas. It is considered inert and well-tolerated, providing a smooth, glossy texture without the tackiness of liquid castor oil.

Hydrogenated castor oil isostearate is an ester of hydrogenated castor oil and isostearic acid used as a smooth, non-greasy emollient in lip products and skin care formulas. It is well-tolerated and provides a comfortable skin feel with good adhesion properties.

Hydrogenated coconut oil is coconut oil that has been fully hydrogenated to convert its unsaturated fatty acids to saturated form, producing a harder, more shelf-stable solid used as a thickener and emollient in balms and anhydrous formulas. It shares the moderate comedogenic potential of coconut oil and is generally well-tolerated in most skin types.

Hydrogenated farnesene is a hydrogenated sesquiterpene hydrocarbon derived from sugarcane fermentation, used as a plant-based squalane alternative with a lightweight, non-greasy emollient skin feel. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated across skin types, and is used in clean-formula moisturizers and serums as a sustainable, non-animal alternative to shark-derived squalane.

Hydrogenated jojoba oil is jojoba wax that has been hydrogenated from its liquid wax ester form to a solid, used as a plant-derived wax and emollient in balms and anhydrous formulas. It retains jojoba's skin compatibility and low comedogenicity in a thickening, structuring format.

Hydrogenated lecithin is a stabilized phospholipid emulsifier derived from soy or sunflower lecithin, used in skin care formulas to create stable emulsions and deliver phospholipids that integrate into the skin's lipid bilayer to support barrier integrity. It is well-tolerated and is considered a skin-compatible, natural-positioned emulsifier.

This entry appears to be an incomplete or truncated ingredient name — 'Hydrogenated Poly' alone is not a recognized standalone INCI ingredient and was likely captured as a data artifact during ingredient list parsing. The full name is probably 'Hydrogenated Polydecene,' 'Hydrogenated Polyisobutene,' or a similar hydrocarbon emollient.

Hydrogenated polydecene is a saturated hydrocarbon emollient derived from polymerized and hydrogenated decene, used as a lightweight, non-greasy alternative to mineral oil with good spreadability and a clean finish. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated across skin types, and used in moisturizers and sun care formulas.

Hydrogenated polyisobutene is a branched-chain hydrocarbon emollient used in lip products, sunscreens, and skin care formulas for its ability to provide a smooth, glossy, non-greasy finish with good occlusive properties. It is considered non-comedogenic, well-tolerated, and is a popular mineral-oil alternative in formulas targeting a cleaner ingredient profile.

Hydrogenated polyisobutene is a branched-chain hydrocarbon emollient used in lip products and skin care for its smooth, glossy finish and mild occlusive moisturizing effect. It is non-comedogenic, well-tolerated across skin types, and is a popular mineral-oil alternative.

Hydrogenated shea butter is shea butter that has been fully or partially hydrogenated to produce a firmer, more stable solid used as an emollient and thickener in balms and richer creams. It retains the fatty acid conditioning profile of natural shea butter and is well-tolerated, though its occlusive nature may not suit acne-prone skin at high concentrations.

Hydrogenated vegetable oil is a generic term for plant oils that have been partially or fully hydrogenated to produce a semi-solid or solid emollient used as a thickener and moisturizer. Tolerance varies depending on the source oil; it is generally well-tolerated, though the degree of comedogenic potential depends on which vegetable oil was hydrogenated.

Hydrolyzed collagen consists of enzymatically broken-down collagen peptides that are too large to penetrate the dermis but form a conditioning, moisture-retaining film on the skin surface. It is well-tolerated, non-sensitizing, and used in hydrating and firming formulas for its surface conditioning and temporary plumping effect — it does not replace lost dermal collagen.

Hydrolyzed corn starch is an enzymatically broken-down corn starch used as a mild thickener, film-former, and absorbent in skincare formulas. It is considered inert, non-sensitizing, and well-tolerated across skin types.

Hydrolyzed glycosaminoglycans are enzymatically broken-down fragments of glycosaminoglycans — the class of polysaccharides that includes hyaluronic acid, chondroitin sulfate, and dermatan sulfate — used in skincare as humectants and skin-conditioning agents. They are well-tolerated and contribute to moisture retention and skin smoothness in hydrating and anti-aging formulas.

Hydrolyzed hyaluronic acid consists of low-molecular-weight HA fragments produced by enzymatic breakdown, which penetrate deeper into the stratum corneum than high-molecular-weight HA to hydrate from within while still providing surface moisture retention. It is exceptionally well-tolerated and is used alongside standard HA in advanced hydrating formulas for multi-depth moisture delivery.

Hydrolyzed jojoba esters are enzymatically broken-down fragments of jojoba wax esters used as conditioning agents that film-form on the skin surface to improve softness and moisture retention. They are well-tolerated, non-comedogenic, and are used in hair and skin care formulas for their lightweight conditioning properties.

Hydrolyzed Myrtus communis (myrtle) leaf extract is an enzymatically processed myrtle extract providing polyphenol antioxidants including myrtucommulone in a more bioavailable, lower-molecular-weight form for improved skin delivery. It is well-tolerated and used in protective formulas for its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties.

Hydrolyzed vegetable protein is a generic term for plant-derived protein fragments from sources such as wheat, soy, or pea that are enzymatically broken down to smaller peptides, used as conditioning agents that strengthen and smooth the skin surface. Tolerance varies by source — those with wheat or soy sensitivities should note the plant origin.
